note: Rates and thresholds change with each year's Finance Act/Budget. Re-verify every rule marked needs_verification:true against ird.gov.np before relying on it for compliance decisions. This file should be re-issued at the start of each new fiscal year.

rules.title: Tax Invoice Header
rules.text: A full Tax Invoice must display the text 'Tax Invoice' (कर बिजक) at the top, in Devanagari, English, or both.
rules.violation_example: Document only says 'Bill' or 'Receipt' with no 'Tax Invoice' label.

rules.title: Invoice Type Classification
rules.text: Determine whether the document is a full Tax Invoice (required fields per FMT rules) or an Abbreviated/Simplified Invoice (used for small retail sales to general consumers below the abbreviated-invoice threshold). The two types have different mandatory fields — buyer PAN and full itemization are not required on an abbreviated invoice.
rules.violation_example: Invoice is being checked against full Tax Invoice rules but is actually a simplified retail receipt.
rules.source: VAT Act / Rules — confirm current abbreviated-invoice threshold

rules.title: Vendor (Supplier) Identity
rules.text: The bill must display the vendor's registered business name, address, and a PAN/VAT number that is exactly 9 numeric digits.
rules.violation_example: Supplier PAN field shows 8 digits, or contains letters.

rules.title: Buyer (Customer) PAN Format
rules.text: Where a buyer PAN is present on the invoice (see FMT-005 for when it is mandatory), it must follow the same format as the vendor PAN: exactly 9 numeric digits. A buyer PAN field containing a name, phone number, or wrong digit count should be flagged even if FMT-005 doesn't strictly require a PAN for that transaction.
rules.violation_example: Customer PAN field reads '9841XXXXXX' (a phone number) or has 7 digits.

rules.title: B2B / High-Value Buyer PAN Requirement
rules.text: Buyer PAN must be collected and shown if the buyer is a registered firm, OR if the transaction amount exceeds NRs. 10,000 — regardless of buyer type.
rules.violation_example: Grand total is NRs. 25,000 to an individual consumer, and the buyer PAN field is blank.

rules.title: Sequential Invoice Numbering
rules.text: Invoice numbers (Bijak Number) must be strictly consecutive and unique per vendor, with no gaps or repeats. This cannot be checked from a single invoice in isolation — it requires comparison against previously seen invoice numbers for the same vendor.
rules.violation_example: Vendor's last invoice was #1042; current invoice is #1044 (gap of one).

rules.title: Bikram Sambat Date
rules.text: Transaction date must be in Nepalese Bikram Sambat (B.S.) format. If only an A.D. date is present, flag it as a format issue (an A.D.-to-B.S. conversion can still be attempted for downstream processing, but the source document is non-compliant).
rules.violation_example: Date shown only as '2026-03-14' with no B.S. equivalent.

rules.title: Line-Item Separation: Taxable vs Exempt vs Zero-Rated
rules.text: Taxable goods (13% VAT), VAT-exempt goods (Schedule 1), and zero-rated goods (mainly exports, 0% VAT) must appear as separate line items/columns, never combined into one total. Note: exempt and zero-rated are NOT the same — zero-rated purchases still allow input VAT credit, exempt ones do not.
rules.violation_example: A single 'Total' line mixes an exempt food item with a taxable electronics item.
rules.source: VAT Act 2052, Schedule 1 — full exempt goods list needs to be sourced and added separately

rules.title: Signature & Seal
rules.text: Invoice must bear the authorized signature and official physical or digital seal of the issuer.
rules.violation_example: No signature or stamp visible anywhere on the document.

rules.title: Credit/Debit Note Reference
rules.text: If the document is a credit note or debit note (an adjustment to a prior sale), it must reference the original invoice number it is adjusting. It should not be treated as a standalone new sale.
rules.violation_example: Document labeled 'Credit Note' has no reference to which original invoice it adjusts.
rules.source: VAT Rules 2053 — confirm exact rule number

rules.title: Standard VAT Calculation
rules.text: VAT Amount = Taxable Amount (sum of taxable lines) × 0.13
rules.violation_example: Taxable amount is 10,000 but VAT shown is 1,200 instead of 1,300.

rules.title: Invoice Grand Total Formula
rules.text: Grand Total = Taxable Amount + VAT Amount + Exempt Amount + Zero-Rated Amount
rules.violation_example: Sum of line components doesn't match the stated grand total.

rules.title: Rounding
rules.text: Line and grand totals should be rounded per standard paisa-rounding convention. A 1-rupee (or smaller) discrepancy from rounding should be treated as acceptable, not flagged as a calculation error.
rules.violation_example: Calculated total is 1300.004, invoice shows 1300 — not a violation.
rules.source: Common practice — confirm exact IRD rounding rule

rules.title: Digital Payment Cash Back (B2C)
rules.text: Consumers paying a retail vendor via digital wallets or cards may be eligible for a cash-back rebate on VAT paid. Rate and eligibility must be confirmed for the current fiscal year before being used as a compliance check.
rules.violation_example: N/A — informational, not a compliance failure if absent.
rules.source: Needs current IRD/government scheme confirmation — rate not verified

rules.title: VAT Credit Ineligible Categories
rules.text: Input VAT credit cannot be claimed on purchases of alcohol, beverages, petrol, diesel, LPG, or entertainment expenses.
rules.violation_example: Purchase invoice for diesel is marked as VAT-credit-eligible in the system.

rules.title: VAT-Registered Service Provider
rules.text: Service payments (consulting, legal, auditing) to a VAT-registered vendor: withhold 1.5% TDS.
rules.violation_example: Consulting invoice from VAT-registered firm shows no TDS line.

rules.title: Non-VAT Service Provider
rules.text: Service payments to a vendor not registered under VAT: withhold 15% TDS.
rules.violation_example: Freelance service invoice with no VAT number shows TDS withheld at 1.5% instead of 15%.

rules.title: Commercial Property Rent (Registered Entity)
rules.text: Renting commercial property from a registered business entity or company: withhold 10% TDS.
rules.violation_example: Office rent invoice from a registered company shows no 10% TDS deduction.

rules.title: Natural Person House Rent
rules.text: Renting property from an individual (natural person): 0% TDS withheld by tenant, since the individual pays local rental tax directly.
rules.violation_example: N/A — confirms no TDS deduction expected.
rules.source: Income Tax Act 2058 — confirm current treatment

rules.title: Vehicle Rental (VAT-Registered Vendor)
rules.text: Renting a vehicle from a VAT-registered company: withhold 1.5% TDS.
rules.violation_example: Vehicle rental invoice from registered company has no TDS line.

rules.title: Vehicle Rental (Non-VAT Vendor)
rules.text: Renting a vehicle from an unregistered operator: withhold 2.5% TDS.
rules.violation_example: Unregistered driver invoice shows 1.5% TDS instead of 2.5%.

rules.title: Business Contract / Supply Threshold
rules.text: For running contracts or supplies, once cumulative payments on a single contract exceed NRs. 50,000, withhold 1.5% TDS on that and all subsequent payments (applies retroactively from the point the threshold is crossed).
rules.violation_example: Third payment on a contract pushes cumulative total past 50,000 but no TDS is withheld going forward.
rules.source: Income Tax Act 2058 — verify exact threshold mechanism (cumulative vs rolling window) against current Finance Act

rules.title: TDS Calculated on Taxable Amount Only
rules.text: TDS is always calculated on the taxable/service amount of the bill — never on the VAT portion.
rules.violation_example: TDS computed on (taxable + VAT) instead of taxable amount alone.

rules.title: Monthly VAT Filing Deadline
rules.text: VAT-registered businesses must submit the monthly VAT return and clear due tax within 25 days of the end of the following Nepali month.
rules.violation_example: Invoice date is more than 25 days into the following month with no filing record.

rules.title: Monthly TDS Filing Deadline
rules.text: Withheld TDS must be deposited and reported to the government within 25 days of the end of the following Nepali month.
rules.violation_example: Same as DDL-001, applied to TDS deposits.
